records.title: Public builds shall use an explicit public-source allowlist
records.normalized_publication_statement: Every public build shall resolve inputs only from the manifest's explicit allowlist and shall reject unlisted inputs.

records.title: Restricted architecture shall reside outside the public repository
records.normalized_publication_statement: Restricted Architecture sources shall be stored in a separately authenticated authority location and shall not be copied into the public source tree.

records.title: Public material shall not disclose real private instances
records.normalized_publication_statement: Public outputs shall omit real instance existence, names, identifiers, memberships, bindings, and active private configuration.

records.title: Secret values shall never enter publication or implementation artifacts
records.normalized_publication_statement: Secret values shall be excluded from every source, build input, prompt, log, fixture, derivative, and archive.

records.title: Canonical publication sources shall be editable and machine-readable
records.normalized_publication_statement: Authoritative publication content shall be maintained as editable Markdown and structured metadata with stable identifiers.

records.title: DOCX and PDF editions shall be generated from canonical sources
records.normalized_publication_statement: DOCX, PDF, modular, and compiled editions shall be reproducible derivatives of the pinned canonical source set.

records.title: Generated editions shall receive page-by-page visual verification
records.normalized_publication_statement: Every generated DOCX and PDF edition shall pass page-by-page visual QA before publication approval.

records.title: Offline archives shall be checksummed and revision-pinned
records.normalized_publication_statement: Every offline archive shall include checksums and exact canonical-source and publication-release identifiers.

records.title: Product implementation shall not begin before IKG0
records.normalized_publication_statement: No DIP product or runtime implementation code shall be produced until IKG0 is formally granted.

records.title: External I/O shall not occur inside long-lived database transactions
records.normalized_publication_statement: External I/O, model calls, and human review shall execute outside long-lived database transactions.

records.title: Every retry shall create a new Attempt
records.normalized_publication_statement: Every retry shall create a new immutable Attempt rather than overwrite prior execution history.

records.title: Submission shall be semantically asynchronous
records.normalized_publication_statement: Every submitted operation shall return durable execution identity even when the initial implementation drains work immediately.

records.title: Uncertain external effects shall require reconciliation
records.normalized_publication_statement: An ambiguous external effect shall block automatic retry until reconciliation establishes a safe next action.

records.title: Processing shall use at-least-once delivery with idempotent effects
records.normalized_publication_statement: DIP processing shall assume at-least-once delivery and require idempotent effects rather than claim exactly-once execution.

records.title: Failures shall be durable, typed, sanitized, and visible
records.normalized_publication_statement: Every processing failure shall be durably recorded with a typed safe representation and surfaced to authorized operators.

records.title: Every observation shall produce a Receipt
records.normalized_publication_statement: Every source observation shall create a Receipt even when content-addressed payload bytes deduplicate.

records.title: Source cursors shall advance only after durable evidence storage
records.normalized_publication_statement: A source cursor shall advance only after the corresponding raw evidence is durably stored and addressable.

records.title: Failed refreshes shall preserve the last accepted good state
records.normalized_publication_statement: A failed refresh shall not replace, invalidate, or obscure the last accepted good state.

records.title: External effects shall originate from authorized Commands
records.normalized_publication_statement: Every external effect shall execute from an authorized durable Command record.

records.title: Every attempted external effect shall produce a durable result state
records.normalized_publication_statement: Every attempted external effect shall produce an Outcome, Failure, or explicitly uncertain state.

records.title: MCP shall not receive privileged direct access
records.normalized_publication_statement: MCP adapters shall receive no direct database, repository, secret, connector, or model-provider access.

records.title: MCP retrieval shall be manifest-first and bounded
records.normalized_publication_statement: MCP retrieval shall begin from stable manifests and enforce explicit bounds on returned context.

records.title: Long-running MCP operations shall return durable Workflow identity
records.normalized_publication_statement: A long-running MCP operation shall return a durable Workflow identity that can be queried through authorized interfaces.

records.title: External invocations shall propagate actor, scope, authority, and causation
records.normalized_publication_statement: Every external invocation shall propagate actor identity, Workspace scope, applicable Private Strategy scope, authority, and causation.

records.title: Lower governance levels shall not weaken higher levels
records.normalized_publication_statement: A lower level of the AI governing hierarchy shall not weaken, bypass, or contradict governance imposed above it.

records.title: Every governed AI execution shall produce an immutable Context Manifest
records.normalized_publication_statement: Every governed AI execution shall persist an immutable Context Manifest describing the exact assembled context and scope.

records.title: Scope filtering shall precede semantic ranking
records.normalized_publication_statement: Retrieval shall enforce authority and scope filtering before semantic relevance ranking.

records.title: Output visibility shall not exceed input visibility
records.normalized_publication_statement: An AI output shall never receive a broader visibility classification than its governing inputs permit.

records.title: Private Strategy context shall require explicit private scope
records.normalized_publication_statement: Private Strategy context shall enter AI execution only under an explicit authorized private execution scope.

records.title: Raw external content shall remain untrusted data
records.normalized_publication_statement: Raw external content shall be treated as untrusted data and excluded from ordinary instruction-bearing reasoning context.

records.title: Persistent AI outputs shall pass local schema validation
records.normalized_publication_statement: An AI-produced candidate shall pass local structural and semantic validation before persistent acceptance.

records.title: Invalid AI outputs shall remain visible but unaccepted
records.normalized_publication_statement: Invalid output shall be preserved as evidence, shall not be partially accepted, and any repair shall occur as a new visible invocation.

records.title: Model substitution and semantic retry shall be explicit
records.normalized_publication_statement: DIP shall prohibit hidden model substitution and hidden semantic retry.

records.title: Models shall receive no privileged direct access
records.normalized_publication_statement: Models shall receive no direct database, repository, connector, MCP, or secret access.

records.title: AI execution budgets shall be pinned policy
records.normalized_publication_statement: Token, cost, time, retry, and tool budgets shall be pinned as part of the governed execution policy.

records.title: Model deployments shall be qualified and conformant
records.normalized_publication_statement: A complete model deployment configuration shall pass task-specific qualification; required conformance failures shall block or suspend activation.

records.title: Conversational information shall enter memory only through governance
records.normalized_publication_statement: Conversational responses shall not automatically become governed memory; persistent conversational information shall enter through the artifact lifecycle.

records.title: Review Items shall bind exact proposal versions
records.normalized_publication_statement: Every Review Item shall reference one exact immutable proposal version.

records.title: Review actions shall enforce exact-version concurrency
records.normalized_publication_statement: Every review action shall enforce optimistic concurrency and exact-version checks.

records.title: Review dispositions shall create immutable Decision Records
records.normalized_publication_statement: Approved, rejected, and changes-requested dispositions shall create immutable Decision Records.

records.title: Compound actions shall preserve separate governed records
records.normalized_publication_statement: A streamlined compound interaction shall preserve every underlying disposition, Command, execution, and Outcome as a separate explicit record.

records.title: Bulk review shall create individual authorized decisions
records.normalized_publication_statement: Bulk review shall create individual Decision Records and shall be limited by scope, authority, compatibility, and risk.

records.title: Authorized interfaces shall preserve governance parity
records.normalized_publication_statement: ChatGPT, MCP, API, Operations Page, and CLI shall invoke the same application commands and queries with identical governance and audit outcomes.

records.title: The private API shall be versioned and schema-governed
records.normalized_publication_statement: The private HTTP/JSON API shall be versioned from `/api/v1`; JSON Schema remains authoritative for derivative API representations.

records.title: Long-running API mutations shall expose durable identity and replay safety
records.normalized_publication_statement: Long-running HTTP commands shall return durable operation identities and support idempotency and stale-state detection.

records.title: Deep links shall be stable, authorized, and non-credential-bearing
records.normalized_publication_statement: Deep links shall preserve stable authorized navigation and shall never function as authorization credentials.

records.title: Initial interfaces shall support mobile review and truthful state
records.normalized_publication_statement: Mobile review and recovery shall be first-release acceptance requirements, and interfaces shall truthfully distinguish accepted, pending, failed, and uncertain states.

records.title: Security shall deny by default
records.normalized_publication_statement: DIP shall enforce resource-centered deny-by-default authorization; absence of an applicable grant means deny.

records.title: Human authentication shall use external OIDC
records.normalized_publication_statement: DIP shall use external OIDC Authorization Code with PKCE and shall not implement a local password database or public self-registration.

records.title: Privileged authentication shall be multifactor-capable
records.normalized_publication_statement: Authentication used for privileged actions shall support multifactor enforcement.

records.title: Browser authentication shall use protected server-side sessions
records.normalized_publication_statement: Browser authentication shall use opaque server-side sessions, secure cookies, and CSRF protection for mutations.

records.title: Remote API and MCP tokens shall be short-lived and audience-bound
records.normalized_publication_statement: Remote API and MCP access tokens shall be short-lived and explicitly audience-bound to DIP.

records.title: Shared human API keys and downstream token pass-through are prohibited
records.normalized_publication_statement: DIP shall prohibit shared human API keys and shall never pass MCP caller tokens through to downstream services.

records.title: Workload classes shall use distinct identities
records.normalized_publication_statement: Schedulers, workers, migrations, and backup processes shall use distinct workload identities.

records.title: Application services shall enforce action- and object-level authorization
records.normalized_publication_statement: Application services shall authorize both the requested action and the targeted object before execution.

records.title: Workspace and platform authority shall not imply Private Strategy access
records.normalized_publication_statement: Workspace access and platform administration shall not automatically grant Private Strategy read authority.

records.title: Protected production data shall use RLS defense in depth
records.normalized_publication_statement: Runtime roles shall not own protected tables or bypass RLS; production scope-sensitive tables shall use PostgreSQL RLS while application authorization remains primary.

records.title: Data classification shall propagate to every derivative
records.normalized_publication_statement: Classification shall follow data into AI context, indexes, logs, exports, and backups.

records.title: Restricted payload encryption shall preserve realm isolation
records.normalized_publication_statement: Restricted payloads shall use application-level envelope encryption, separately scoped Workspace and Private Strategy keys, and realm-scoped deduplication.

records.title: Production secrets and environments shall remain separated
records.normalized_publication_statement: Production secrets shall live outside application configuration and storage; development, test, and production shall use separate data, credentials, keys, clients, and endpoints.

records.title: Security audit shall be distinct, append-oriented, and minimized
records.normalized_publication_statement: Security audit records shall remain distinct from diagnostic logs and the domain Event Ledger and shall be append-oriented and content-minimized.

records.title: Remote production exposure shall satisfy the security readiness posture
records.normalized_publication_statement: Only the authenticated edge may be remotely exposed; a threat model, ASVS Level 2 posture, appropriate step-up controls, fail-closed behavior, and the security readiness gate are required before remote production use.

records.title: Testing shall be layered, risk-based, and contract-driven
records.normalized_publication_statement: DIP testing shall use layered risk-based coverage with executable contracts for authoritative schemas and ports.

records.title: PostgreSQL and RLS behavior shall be tested on PostgreSQL 18
records.normalized_publication_statement: Persistence and RLS tests shall run against real PostgreSQL 18; database mocks and SQLite shall not substitute for behavioral tests.

records.title: Workflow tests shall cover interruption and uncertainty
records.normalized_publication_statement: Workflow tests shall cover interruption, duplication, replay, stale leases, and uncertain effects.

records.title: Every connector shall pass shared semantic contracts
records.normalized_publication_statement: Every connector implementation shall pass the shared connector semantic contract suite.

records.title: Interfaces shall share use-case acceptance scenarios
records.normalized_publication_statement: MCP, API, UI, and CLI shall pass shared use-case acceptance scenarios.

records.title: Deterministic AI tests and live-model conformance shall remain separate
records.normalized_publication_statement: Deterministic AI integration shall use a fake model adapter; live-model conformance shall run separately from ordinary deterministic CI.

records.title: Public CI shall exclude private fixtures
records.normalized_publication_statement: Public CI shall use synthetic or sanitized fixtures, while private-authority and Workspace fixtures remain outside the public repository.

records.title: Required quality and conformance failures shall block activation
records.normalized_publication_statement: A required quality or conformance gate failure shall block activation.

records.title: Telemetry shall remain distinct, correlated, allowlisted, and low-cardinality
records.normalized_publication_statement: Telemetry shall remain distinct from authoritative records, propagate correlation, use allowlisted fields, prohibit private content, and exclude high-cardinality domain or security identities from metric labels.

records.title: Health and freshness states shall remain distinct
records.normalized_publication_statement: Liveness, readiness, dependency health, and product freshness shall be measured and reported separately.

records.title: Failed work shall never disappear silently
records.normalized_publication_statement: Failed messages and artifacts shall never be silently discarded; irrecoverable work shall enter a visible terminal or quarantine state.

records.title: Reconciliation shall be explicit and history-preserving
records.normalized_publication_statement: Reconciliation shall execute as a governed Workflow and shall never silently rewrite authoritative history.

records.title: Operational Conformance shall produce actionable evidence
records.normalized_publication_statement: Operational Conformance shall run on demand and on schedule, produce versioned evidence-bearing reports, block or suspend on critical failure, and be supported by an incident taxonomy and runbooks before production.

records.title: Production deployment shall use immutable image and manifest identity
records.normalized_publication_statement: Production shall deploy one immutable OCI image by digest and create an immutable Deployment Manifest for every deployment.

records.title: Infrastructure and environments shall be controlled and isolated
records.normalized_publication_statement: Nonsecret infrastructure shall be represented as code; local, CI, and production shall be isolated; production deployment shall require protected-mainline CI and environment approval.

records.title: Production migrations shall be forward-only and deployment-blocking
records.normalized_publication_statement: Production migrations shall favor forward-only expand-and-contract changes; migration failure shall block deployment, and code/configuration rollback is preferred over database down-migration.

records.title: Deployment and governed activation shall remain separate
records.normalized_publication_statement: Code deployment shall not automatically activate new capabilities, AIOP versions, models, or external effects.

records.title: ChatGPT/MCP launch shall require the compatibility spike and authenticated posture
records.normalized_publication_statement: Architecture Spike 001 shall be completed before ChatGPT/MCP launch is confirmed; no-auth MCP is prohibited, and the private Operations Page/API remains the fallback.

records.title: Backups shall satisfy declared retention, replication, RPO, and RTO
records.normalized_publication_statement: Database and payload backups shall use the approved retention and cross-provider replication posture and shall satisfy the declared recovery objectives.

records.title: Restores shall be isolated and exercised
records.normalized_publication_statement: Restores shall occur in a new isolated environment; database restore tests shall run monthly and full recovery exercises quarterly.

records.title: Key recovery material shall remain provider-separated
records.normalized_publication_statement: Key recovery material shall remain separate from production providers.

records.title: Retention and deletion shall be governed and backup-aware
records.normalized_publication_statement: Accepted authoritative history shall follow governed retention, shorter-lived diagnostic/rejected material shall use explicit classes, and deletion shall remain pending while required backups persist.

records.title: Production correction and alerting shall use governed operations
records.normalized_publication_statement: Monitoring shall begin with critical alerts, and routine production corrections shall use application workflows rather than direct database edits.

records.title: Scaling shall require measured evidence
records.normalized_publication_statement: Scaling shall proceed vertically first; service extraction shall require measured evidence.

records.title: Production launch shall require an accepted readiness assessment
records.normalized_publication_statement: Production launch shall require a formal Production Readiness Assessment and immutable approval Decision Record.

records.title: Production cost shall remain inside the approved envelope
records.normalized_publication_statement: Initial production infrastructure cost shall remain within the approved architecture envelope or receive explicit approval.

records.title: The release pipeline shall build, test, scan, and publish
records.normalized_publication_statement: The protected release pipeline shall build, test, scan, and publish the immutable production image.

records.title: The first vertical slice shall exercise the full governed lifecycle
records.normalized_publication_statement: The initial league-state intelligence slice shall traverse the complete governed execution lifecycle.

records.title: The first slice shall include bounded AI and exclude market-valued advice
records.normalized_publication_statement: The first slice shall include one governed AI brief but shall exclude market-valued trade advice.

records.title: The first slice shall demonstrate governed internal action without external mutation
records.normalized_publication_statement: The first slice shall demonstrate Review → Decision → internal Command → Outcome without mutating the external first-domain platform.

records.title: The initial first-domain source shall remain read-only
records.normalized_publication_statement: The initial first-domain source shall remain read-only in the initial release.

records.title: Private fixtures shall stay private and public tests shall use synthetic equivalents
records.normalized_publication_statement: Private Workspace fixtures shall remain outside the public repository, and structurally equivalent synthetic fixtures shall support public testing.

records.title: Value-dependent recommendations shall wait for qualified market evidence
records.normalized_publication_statement: Value-dependent recommendations shall remain disabled until the market-intelligence gate passes with the required qualified and comparison sources.

records.title: The private API and Operations Page shall remain the authoritative launch fallback
records.normalized_publication_statement: If ChatGPT/MCP compatibility is not confirmed, the private API and Operations Page shall provide the authoritative launch interface without an unauthenticated workaround.

records.title: Build work shall proceed through measurable gates
records.normalized_publication_statement: Implementation work shall progress through evidence-based gates rather than a speculative calendar.

records.title: Architecture Publication shall precede implementation
records.normalized_publication_statement: Architecture Publication shall complete before product implementation begins.

records.title: Cross-cutting acceptance obligations shall remain continuous
records.normalized_publication_statement: Security, testing, observability, documentation, and recovery shall remain continuous acceptance requirements.

records.title: Additional infrastructure shall require evidence or a new ADR
records.normalized_publication_statement: No additional infrastructure component shall be introduced without measured need or a new ADR.

records.title: Every slice shall preserve governing architecture
records.normalized_publication_statement: A narrowed implementation slice shall still exercise the applicable governing lifecycle and continuous acceptance obligations.

records.title: Public test assets shall be structurally representative and non-private
records.normalized_publication_statement: Public contract and acceptance tests shall use structurally representative synthetic assets without importing private source material.

records.title: Value-dependent analyzers shall abstain before qualification
records.normalized_publication_statement: Value-dependent analyzers shall abstain rather than substitute unsupported intuition before the market-intelligence gate passes.

records.title: Scheduling and infrastructure shall be evidence-driven
records.normalized_publication_statement: Calendar commitment and infrastructure expansion shall follow measured implementation evidence rather than speculation.

records.title: DIP shall use a modular monorepo as the public-reproducible source of truth
records.normalized_publication_statement: DIP implementation shall use a modular monorepo as its public-reproducible engineering source of truth.

records.title: Private authority and runtime records shall remain outside ordinary source control
records.normalized_publication_statement: Real Workspace and Private Strategy authority shall come from an external private-authoritative source, and runtime operational records shall live in governed persistence.

records.title: Architectural zones shall remain distinct and composable
records.normalized_publication_statement: Core, AIOP, application, infrastructure, Domain Packages, Workspaces, and Private Strategy shall remain distinct; applications compose them without owning reusable canonical logic.

records.title: Dependencies shall point inward to Core contracts
records.normalized_publication_statement: Core shall remain independent, AIOP and Domain Packages may depend inward on Core, and infrastructure shall implement inward-facing ports.

records.title: Dependency boundaries shall be machine-enforced
records.normalized_publication_statement: CI shall enforce dependency boundaries, and cross-Domain-Package composition shall use a future governed mechanism rather than direct imports.

records.title: Authoritative schemas and generated assets shall remain distinguishable and reproducible
records.normalized_publication_statement: JSON Schema shall remain authoritative; language models shall derive from or validate against it, and committed generated files shall be reproducible and drift-checked.

records.title: Migration concerns shall remain separated
records.normalized_publication_statement: Database, artifact, schema, and configuration migrations shall remain distinct concerns.

records.title: The engineering foundation shall start with minimal services and stable CI commands
records.normalized_publication_statement: Local and test environments shall be implemented first with a minimal service footprint and one required CI workflow invoking stable repository commands.

records.title: Architecture and conformance assets shall remain repository-adjacent
records.normalized_publication_statement: Architecture, AIOP, the Constitution, schemas, implementation, and conformance tests shall live together in the repository.

records.title: The first slice shall prove governance rather than bypass it
records.normalized_publication_statement: The first vertical slice shall exercise Recommendation and Review Service governance rather than begin as an unstructured domain script.

records.title: The primary Python runtime shall be pinned and bounded
records.normalized_publication_statement: DIP shall use CPython 3.14, support the `3.14.x` series, and reject unreviewed `3.15+` execution.

records.title: Python workspace and dependency state shall be reproducible
records.normalized_publication_statement: `uv` shall manage Python, environments, dependencies, workspace membership, and the committed lockfile; the root remains a virtual workspace.

records.title: Importable packages shall use explicit layout and dependencies
records.normalized_publication_statement: Importable packages shall use `src` layout and explicitly declared internal dependencies.

records.title: JSON Schema shall remain authoritative across Python boundaries
records.normalized_publication_statement: Draft 2020-12 JSON Schema shall govern persisted and exchanged contracts; Python validation and Pydantic boundary models shall not replace schema authority.

records.title: Canonical domain objects shall remain framework-independent
records.normalized_publication_statement: Core and domain entities shall remain framework-independent; persistence, HTTP, and provider SDK representations shall not become canonical domain models.

records.title: Python implementation shall be strictly typed and statically checked
records.normalized_publication_statement: Python shall be typed from inception using strict checking, linting, formatting, and tests, with `Any` constrained to unvalidated external boundaries.

records.title: Initial interaction shall use a portable CLI before the API slice
records.normalized_publication_statement: The initial user interface shall be a cross-platform CLI using `uv`-managed entry points; FastAPI shall be introduced only when required by the API slice.

records.title: Application I/O shall be async while deterministic domain logic remains synchronous
records.normalized_publication_statement: Application ports shall support asynchronous I/O, deterministic domain logic shall remain synchronous, and initial workflow execution shall be in-process behind durable records.

records.title: PostgreSQL integration shall use the approved relational stack and reviewed migrations
records.normalized_publication_statement: PostgreSQL 18, SQLAlchemy 2.x, Psycopg 3, and Alembic shall form the relational baseline; SQLite shall not substitute for integration tests, and generated migrations require review.

records.title: Model invocation shall remain provider-neutral and deterministically testable
records.normalized_publication_statement: DIP shall own provider-neutral model and conformance contracts, avoid foundational third-party agent frameworks, and provide a deterministic mock model provider.

records.title: Linux shall be canonical while developer environments remain portable
records.normalized_publication_statement: Linux shall be canonical for CI and production; Windows, macOS, and Linux remain supported for development, using OCI containers and Compose for service baselines.

records.title: Configuration formats shall have declared roles
records.normalized_publication_statement: TOML shall govern Python tooling, YAML shall serve human-authored JSON-compatible manifests and policies, and JSON shall govern artifacts and machine exchange.

records.title: Environment variables shall remain bootstrap inputs
records.normalized_publication_statement: Environment variables and `.env` files shall not become full policy stores.

records.title: Persistence shall begin in one PostgreSQL authority with distinct data categories
records.normalized_publication_statement: DIP shall begin with one PostgreSQL database, logical ownership and scope boundaries, and distinct immutable-artifact, operational-state, and rebuildable-projection categories without universal event sourcing.

records.title: Identity, hashes, and temporal semantics shall remain distinct
records.normalized_publication_statement: DIP identity, external identifiers, artifact/version identity, payload hashes, and recording, observation, acceptance, and effective time shall remain explicitly distinct.

records.title: Accepted artifact history shall be immutable and precisely attributable
records.normalized_publication_statement: Accepted versions shall be immutable; changes create new versions, retain lifecycle history, identify schema and provenance, and link exact versions.

records.title: Receipt, validation, and acceptance shall remain separate
records.normalized_publication_statement: Source receipt, validation, and artifact acceptance shall remain separate stages, and invalid inputs shall remain visible without becoming accepted artifacts.

records.title: Artifact acceptance shall be atomic
records.normalized_publication_statement: Acceptance shall occur in one explicit transaction that atomically creates required provenance, lifecycle, and outbox records.

records.title: Payload storage shall remain hybrid, content-addressed, authority-linked, and scope-bounded
records.normalized_publication_statement: Small structured payloads may use JSONB; large or binary payloads use the payload abstraction, database references determine authority, writes precede acceptance, orphans are removable, and private deduplication is scope-bounded.

records.title: The event ledger shall be append-only without requiring full event sourcing
records.normalized_publication_statement: DIP shall maintain append-only transition evidence without making event replay the sole state authority.

records.title: Workflow identity and retry history shall be durable
records.normalized_publication_statement: Trigger, Workflow, Stage, Job, and Attempt shall have durable identities, and retries shall create new Attempts.

records.title: Review, decision, recommendation, command, and outcome records shall remain distinct
records.normalized_publication_statement: Review records and immutable Decision Records shall remain distinct, and Recommendations, Commands, and Outcomes shall be separate first-class records.

records.title: Application use cases shall own transactions and exclude long external I/O
records.normalized_publication_statement: The Unit of Work shall be owned by application use cases; repositories and domain objects shall not commit, and long external I/O shall remain outside the transaction.

records.title: Propagation and mutable state shall be replay-safe
records.normalized_publication_statement: Asynchronous propagation shall use an outbox, retried consumers shall deduplicate, operations shall use idempotency keys, and mutable state shall detect conflicts.

records.title: Initial Memory, Knowledge, and Intelligence shall use relational governed structures
records.normalized_publication_statement: Initial Memory and Knowledge shall use PostgreSQL artifacts, events, projections, nodes, edges, assertions, and evidence; graph and embedding infrastructure remain unnecessary initially.

records.title: Authoritative scope shall be explicit and strengthened before shared production
records.normalized_publication_statement: Scoped rows shall carry Workspace and Private Strategy scope; application enforcement is acceptable locally, while shared production requires defense-in-depth database isolation.

records.title: Governed history shall not use generalized destructive deletion
records.normalized_publication_statement: Governed artifacts shall preserve history; projections may be rebuilt, while production lifecycle controls require their approved policies.

records.title: Migrations shall be ordered, explicit, reviewed, tested, and compatibility-enforced
records.normalized_publication_statement: DIP shall use one ordered Alembic lineage; migrations shall not run on import, generated changes require review, CI shall test empty-database application, and incompatible startup shall fail visibly.

records.title: Configuration shall be governed as an explicit authority graph
records.normalized_publication_statement: Configuration shall preserve distinct authority planes, explicit field ownership, and a machine-readable authority registry; unauthorized values shall fail compilation.

records.title: Lower configuration layers shall only narrow authority
records.normalized_publication_statement: Lower layers and runtime constraints may reduce capability but shall never exceed higher-level authority ceilings.

records.title: Public, private, runtime, and loaded configuration sources shall remain separated and bounded
records.normalized_publication_statement: Public configuration shall remain in the repository, real authority outside it, activations in persistence, and private loading behind a bounded provider interface that rejects scanning and path escape.

records.title: Configuration shall be schema-governed, compiled, deterministic, and historically activated
records.normalized_publication_statement: Every configuration family shall have strict schema identity and compatibility; compilation shall validate authority and capability, produce deterministic canonical bundles, and preserve activated history.

records.title: Configuration combination shall be explicit and restrictive
records.normalized_publication_statement: Unrestricted deep merge is prohibited; replacement is default, lists replace, permissions and review combine restrictively, ceilings only narrow, and deletion requires an explicit operation.

records.title: Configuration validation and activation shall remain separate and atomic
records.normalized_publication_statement: A valid candidate shall not activate automatically; activation is atomic, and failure preserves the prior valid bundle.

records.title: Workflows and governed records shall retain exact activation references
records.normalized_publication_statement: Every workflow shall pin its activation, in-flight work shall not silently change, and recommendations through outcomes shall retain relevant activation identity.

records.title: Configuration changes shall be impact-classified and reviewed appropriately
records.normalized_publication_statement: High-impact activation shall require Review Service authority; local automation may only activate low-risk changes and cannot approve command-enabling or constitutional changes.

records.title: Environment profiles shall control deployment behavior without granting domain authority
records.normalized_publication_statement: Environment profiles shall select deployment behavior and may narrow ceilings, but cannot grant authority or be relabeled to production without recompilation and validation; environment variables remain bootstrap-only.

records.title: Secrets shall remain typed references resolved lazily at capability boundaries
records.normalized_publication_statement: Authoritative configuration shall contain typed secret references, never plaintext values; dedicated resolvers shall resolve lazily, redact values everywhere, preserve safe audit metadata, support rotation, and fail visibly without unauthorized fallback.

records.title: Private Strategy loading shall require explicit isolated authority
records.normalized_publication_statement: Private Strategy shall require explicit Workspace and principal scope, never silently fall back or combine principals, automatically narrow visibility, and remain outside implicit operator authority.

records.title: Configuration failure and degraded operation shall remain visible and explainable
records.normalized_publication_statement: Invalid candidates and missing authority shall remain visible while preserving valid activation; allowed degraded operation shall be bounded, freshness/provenance queryable, secrets redacted, and CI shall verify compilation, authority, examples, and hygiene.

records.title: Normative inventory statements shall require demonstrable approval
records.normalized_publication_statement: Only E1 accepted/formally approved artifacts, E2 proposal-and-explicit-approval pairs, and E3 approved summaries or authoritative handoffs may establish or confirm normative publication content; E4 remains historical context and E5 is prohibited from normativity.

records.title: Approved summaries shall not displace available detailed source language
records.normalized_publication_statement: Approved summaries may confirm lifecycle state but shall not replace available detailed authority language.

records.title: Missing evidence shall remain a publication gap
records.normalized_publication_statement: Missing, incomplete, or uncertain evidence shall remain explicitly recorded and shall not be filled through inference.

records.title: Publication identifiers shall be permanent, non-semantic, and preserved
records.normalized_publication_statement: Record identifiers shall remain permanent and location-independent; descriptive placement and authority remain metadata, existing identifiers remain unchanged, and approved non-ADR decisions are not retroactively converted into ADRs.

records.title: Every normalized decision shall retain source and publication layers
records.normalized_publication_statement: Every normalized decision shall preserve a faithful approved-source layer and a distinct concise architectural-publication layer.

records.title: Normalization shall be limited to approved editorial transformations
records.normalized_publication_statement: Normalization may perform only terminology, decomposition, scaffolding removal, requirement/rationale separation, and traceability-metadata transformations that preserve meaning.

records.title: Normalization shall not alter obligation, lifecycle, activation, history, or vendor scope
records.normalized_publication_statement: Editorial normalization shall preserve obligation strength, approval state, modal force, deferral and conditional status, historical terminology, and approved vendor or implementation scope.

records.title: Meaning-dependent compound decisions shall remain atomic
records.normalized_publication_statement: A compound approved decision shall remain one record whenever decomposition would alter its meaning.

records.title: The publication corpus shall use the approved status taxonomy consistently
records.normalized_publication_statement: Every publication record shall use the approved lifecycle taxonomy, and Deferred shall retain governed architectural intent rather than imply optionality or rejection.

records.title: Every Blueprint capability shall carry an approved activation classification
records.normalized_publication_statement: Each Blueprint capability shall be classified by its approved initial, slice-specific, portability, conditional, deferred, or reassessment activation posture.

records.title: P0 shall produce nine controlled inventories that seed Foundations and Blueprint
records.normalized_publication_statement: P0 shall control the nine approved inventory families and use them as traceability inputs to Foundations and Blueprint, not as competing architectural authority.

records.title: Design history and formal decision history shall remain distinct
records.normalized_publication_statement: Architectural Foundations may synthesize design evolution, while formal decision history shall preserve exact authority and provenance; neither substitutes for the other.

records.title: Full Foundations drafting shall require PG0 passage
records.normalized_publication_statement: Full Architectural Foundations drafting shall not begin until PG0 is formally evaluated and passed against its approved criteria.

records.title: PG1 criterion — all chapters are drafted from approved or properly cited sources
records.normalized_publication_statement: PG1 shall not pass until this condition is verified: all chapters are drafted from approved or properly cited sources.

records.title: PG1 criterion — historical narrative and normative decisions are distinguishable
records.normalized_publication_statement: PG1 shall not pass until this condition is verified: historical narrative and normative decisions are distinguishable.

records.title: PG1 criterion — the companion-intelligence framing is understandable without the Blueprint
records.normalized_publication_statement: PG1 shall not pass until this condition is verified: the companion-intelligence framing is understandable without the Blueprint.

records.title: PG1 criterion — the relationship between AI and DIP avoids model-as-authority ambiguity
records.normalized_publication_statement: PG1 shall not pass until this condition is verified: the relationship between AI and DIP avoids model-as-authority ambiguity.

records.title: PG1 criterion — the first-domain story does not narrow the platform’s governing purpose
records.normalized_publication_statement: PG1 shall not pass until this condition is verified: the first-domain story does not narrow the platform’s governing purpose.

records.title: PG1 criterion — no private Workspace or Private Strategy information is exposed
records.normalized_publication_statement: PG1 shall not pass until this condition is verified: no private Workspace or Private Strategy information is exposed.

records.title: PG1 criterion — every architectural summary links to its normative source
records.normalized_publication_statement: PG1 shall not pass until this condition is verified: every architectural summary links to its normative source.

records.normalized_publication_statement: PG1 shall not pass until this condition is verified: deferred capabilities are not presented as implemented.

records.title: PG1 criterion — unsupported novelty or market claims have been removed or qualified
records.normalized_publication_statement: PG1 shall not pass until this condition is verified: unsupported novelty or market claims have been removed or qualified.

records.title: PG1 criterion — terminology matches the P0 register
records.normalized_publication_statement: PG1 shall not pass until this condition is verified: terminology matches the P0 register.

records.normalized_publication_statement: PG1 shall not pass until this condition is verified: word and PDF renderings pass visual review.

records.normalized_publication_statement: PG3 shall not pass until this condition is verified: every retrospective record has evidence and reconstruction metadata.

records.normalized_publication_statement: PG3 shall not pass until this condition is verified: every governing decision links to the Blueprint.

records.normalized_publication_statement: PG3 shall not pass until this condition is verified: every normative Blueprint area links back to governing decisions.

records.title: PG3 criterion — public records contain no private evidence or Workspace data
records.normalized_publication_statement: PG3 shall not pass until this condition is verified: public records contain no private evidence or Workspace data.

records.title: PG3 criterion — Architecture v1.0 can pin the exact decision corpus
records.normalized_publication_statement: PG3 shall not pass until this condition is verified: architecture v1.0 can pin the exact decision corpus.

records.normalized_publication_statement: PG4 shall not pass until this condition is verified: PG0 through PG3 have passed.

records.normalized_publication_statement: PG4 shall not pass until this condition is verified: every required release component is included.

records.normalized_publication_statement: PG4 shall not pass until this condition is verified: all six review tracks are complete.

records.normalized_publication_statement: PG4 shall not pass until this condition is verified: checksums verify.

records.normalized_publication_statement: PG4 shall not pass until public-reference and restricted-stewardship editions contain only appropriately classified material, live Private Authority remains external, and Secrets are excluded.

records.normalized_publication_statement: PG6 shall not pass until this condition is verified: the human implementation-start checklist is ready.

records.title: Preserve the governing hierarchy
records.normalized_publication_statement: DIP shall preserve the Core → Domain Package → Workspace → Private Strategy hierarchy.

records.title: Preserve the four information layers
records.normalized_publication_statement: DIP shall distinguish Memory, Knowledge, Intelligence, and Interaction authorities.

records.title: Enforce the information lifecycle
records.normalized_publication_statement: Accepted information shall follow Receive → Validate → Accept → Version → Propagate → Observe.

records.title: Enforce the workflow hierarchy
records.normalized_publication_statement: Durable workflows shall preserve Trigger → Workflow → Stage → Job → Attempt.

records.title: Preserve semantic message distinctions
records.normalized_publication_statement: Domain Events, Orchestration Signals, Commands, and Outcomes shall remain distinct.

records.title: Preserve artifact-class distinctions
records.normalized_publication_statement: Raw, Normalized, Canonical, and Derived artifacts shall remain distinct.

records.title: Require the governed execution chain
records.normalized_publication_statement: A Recommendation shall not directly cause an external effect without Decision Record, Command, and Outcome records.

records.title: Isolate Private Strategy independently
records.normalized_publication_statement: Private Strategy shall remain a separately authorized and isolated realm.

records.title: Use governed schema contracts
records.normalized_publication_statement: Canonical schemas shall use JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 and preserve layer ownership, strict validation, compatibility, and fixtures.

records.title: Separate stable and version identity
records.normalized_publication_statement: Stable identity shall remain distinct from immutable version identity.

records.title: Retain provenance and lineage
records.normalized_publication_statement: Accepted and derived information shall retain distinct provenance and lineage evidence.

records.title: Declare one authority per durable fact
records.normalized_publication_statement: Every durable information category shall have exactly one logical authority owner.

records.title: Preserve authoritative immutability
records.normalized_publication_statement: Accepted artifacts, semantic ledger records, graph generations, registry versions, and resolution records shall be immutable.

records.title: Use replay-safe propagation
records.normalized_publication_statement: Cross-authority propagation shall use durable outbox and idempotent consumption patterns or equivalent evidence-preserving mechanisms.

records.title: Preserve bitemporal interpretation
records.normalized_publication_statement: Historical interpretation shall preserve recorded and effective time without rewriting prior accepted state.

records.title: Govern registry activation and resolution
records.normalized_publication_statement: Schema and configuration registration, activation, dependency resolution, and resolution evidence shall be authoritative and versioned.

records.title: Keep graph engines rebuildable
records.normalized_publication_statement: Graph storage and traversal engines shall be replaceable and rebuildable from authoritative inputs.

records.title: Keep derivatives non-authoritative
records.normalized_publication_statement: Caches, indexes, projections, replicas, and generated views shall not become exclusive authority.

records.title: Require reconciled recovery
records.normalized_publication_statement: Recovery shall include authoritative reconciliation and isolation verification before normal service resumes.

records.title: Require qualified technology and conformance
records.normalized_publication_statement: Technology selection, deployment, security, delivery, and readiness shall satisfy approved evidence and conformance gates.

records.title: Keep first-domain semantics out of Core
records.normalized_publication_statement: First-domain semantics shall remain in the Domain Package rather than DIP Core.

records.title: Trace initial scope to highest-value journeys
records.normalized_publication_statement: Initial first-domain work shall trace to approved user journeys and usefulness outcomes.

records.title: Qualify market evidence before dependent intelligence
records.normalized_publication_statement: Market-dependent intelligence shall remain inactive until operational and comparative sources are qualified.

records.title: Retain source-specific provenance and freshness
records.normalized_publication_statement: Every external first-domain source shall retain provider, methodology, format, observation, and freshness evidence.

records.title: Maintain governed interface parity
records.normalized_publication_statement: ChatGPT and the Operations Page shall preserve identical Review Service, authorization, and audit semantics.

records.title: Keep conversation bounded as a trigger
records.normalized_publication_statement: Conversation may initiate workflows but shall not create automatic memory, authority, decision, or mutation.

records.title: Require review before consequential mutation
records.normalized_publication_statement: Consequential mutation shall remain behind the approved review and decision boundary.

records.title: Provide minimum governed operations
records.normalized_publication_statement: The initial private Operations Page shall support review, refresh, and workflow inspection.

records.title: Preserve governance in the low-cost posture
records.normalized_publication_statement: Local-first or low-cost deployment shall not weaken authority, isolation, durability, recovery, or audit.

records.title: Preserve Workspace and Private Strategy isolation
records.normalized_publication_statement: Workspace and Private Strategy data, membership, binding, and sharing shall remain explicitly authorized.

records.title: Separate operational activation from portability validation
records.normalized_publication_statement: The first operational Workspace and portability Workspace shall remain distinct governed boundaries.

records.title: Require a separate readiness decision
records.normalized_publication_statement: Completion of G1 shall not alone authorize implementation planning; the separate viability and readiness review must pass.

records.title: Preserve first-domain alignment with the approved vision
records.normalized_publication_statement: The first-domain implementation profile shall remain aligned with the original DIP vision and governing architecture.

records.title: Sequence work for disciplined single-implementer delivery
records.normalized_publication_statement: Implementation planning shall sequence the initial system so one disciplined implementer can deliver it without bypassing governance.

records.title: Remain inside the Minimum Operating Profile envelope
records.normalized_publication_statement: The initial implementation profile shall remain inside the approved cost, infrastructure, storage, and compute envelope.

records.title: Complete AIOP before implementation planning
records.normalized_publication_statement: AIOP shall be complete and approved before implementation planning begins.

records.title: Apply Version 1 scope discipline
records.normalized_publication_statement: Version 1 shall reduce delivery breadth without reducing or bypassing the governing architecture.

records.title: Keep readiness approval distinct from coding authorization
records.normalized_publication_statement: The viability review shall authorize implementation planning only; product coding requires the separate publication and implementation authorization sequence.

records.title: Enforce the AI governance hierarchy
records.normalized_publication_statement: AI execution shall preserve Foundation Model → AIOP → Domain Package → Workspace → Private Strategy specialization and authority order.

records.title: Version AIOP independently of model providers
records.normalized_publication_statement: AIOP shall remain provider-neutral, independently versioned, and enforceable across qualified model deployments.

records.title: Preserve human agency and controlling authority
records.normalized_publication_statement: AI behavior shall improve human judgment, retain evidence-based dissent, and preserve controlling human and platform authority.

records.title: Label material reasoning modes
records.normalized_publication_statement: Material reasoning shall distinguish observation, interpretation, inference, forecast, judgment, and recommendation.

records.title: Evaluate evidence fitness
records.normalized_publication_statement: Evidence shall be evaluated for provenance, freshness, applicability, and decision fitness.

records.title: Preserve conflicting evidence
records.normalized_publication_statement: Materially conflicting evidence shall remain visible and its effect on conclusions shall be explained.

records.title: Represent confidence at three levels
records.normalized_publication_statement: Evidence confidence, inference confidence, and decision confidence shall remain distinct.

records.title: Provide decision-relevant explanations
records.normalized_publication_statement: Explanations shall expose decision-relevant evidence, assumptions, uncertainty, alternatives, and tradeoffs.

records.title: Structure material recommendations
records.normalized_publication_statement: Material recommendations shall record goals, alternatives, evidence, assumptions, confidence, tradeoffs, validity, and authority status.

records.title: Permit governed abstention
records.normalized_publication_statement: AI may abstain, recommend no action, condition analysis, or request further evidence when support is insufficient.

records.title: Deny authority to retrieved content
records.normalized_publication_statement: Retrieved content shall be treated as evidence and shall never grant authority or override governing instructions.

records.title: Keep recommendations non-executable
records.normalized_publication_statement: Recommendations shall remain non-executable and shall proceed only through Decision Record, Command, and Outcome controls.

records.title: Make DIP authoritative for durable memory
records.normalized_publication_statement: DIP shall own durable memory and continuity independently of model or session state.

records.title: Separate context and memory lifecycle states
records.normalized_publication_statement: Session context, working context, memory candidates, and accepted memory shall remain distinct states.

records.title: Review durable-memory promotion
records.normalized_publication_statement: Conversation-derived memory shall become durable only through governed Review Service acceptance.

records.title: Authorize every context item
records.normalized_publication_statement: Context inclusion shall be authorized for actor, scope, realm, purpose, permissions, and governed task.

records.title: Resolve scope before mutation
records.normalized_publication_statement: A scoped mutation shall not proceed without a resolved and authorized Workspace and information realm.

records.title: Prevent silent cross-Workspace transfer
records.normalized_publication_statement: Workspace information shall not cross Workspace boundaries without explicit governed authorization and provenance.

records.title: Independently protect Private Strategy
records.normalized_publication_statement: Private Strategy shall retain independent authorization and information-isolation controls.

records.title: Inherit information boundaries in derived outputs
records.normalized_publication_statement: Derived outputs shall inherit applicable source information boundaries.

records.title: Bound AI initiative independently of authority
records.normalized_publication_statement: AI initiative shall use explicit levels and shall never confer mutation or execution authority by itself.

records.title: Require review before consequential mutation
records.normalized_publication_statement: Consequential mutation and external action shall remain inside explicit policy, authority, and Review Service controls.

records.title: Govern MCP, tools, commands, and external representation
records.normalized_publication_statement: AI use of MCP, tools, commands, and external communication shall be capability-bounded, attributable, and auditable.

records.title: Record and reconcile execution outcomes
records.normalized_publication_statement: Execution outcomes and uncertain external effects shall be recorded and reconciled before retry or further action.

records.title: Preserve interface governance parity
records.normalized_publication_statement: Every authorized interface shall preserve equivalent authority, review, status, and audit semantics.

records.title: Preserve attribution, privacy, and accurate status
records.normalized_publication_statement: Multi-user differences shall remain attributable; shared and private interactions shall remain distinct; lifecycle status shall be accurate and non-deceptive.

records.title: Make failure and learning governed state
records.normalized_publication_statement: Protected failures shall be visible and fail closed; recovery, fallback, correction, learning, and adaptation shall remain governed and history-preserving.

records.title: Require deployment-level conformance and constitutional immutability
records.normalized_publication_statement: A complete versioned deployment shall pass AIOP conformance, and AI execution shall not amend or weaken the DIP AI Constitution.

records.title: Treat DIP as the governed environment around replaceable models
records.normalized_publication_statement: DIP shall remain the governed operating environment around replaceable reasoning models rather than a model-centric AI application.

records.title: Keep models outside durable authority and system-of-record roles
records.normalized_publication_statement: A foundation model shall not serve as DIP's database, memory authority, workflow engine, authorization authority, or system of record.

records.title: Keep durable platform capabilities model-independent
records.normalized_publication_statement: Identity, continuity, memory, knowledge, governance, workflow, review, controlled execution, and audit history shall remain durable DIP capabilities independent of model state.

records.title: Preserve model, AIOP, domain, Workspace, and Private Strategy separation
records.normalized_publication_statement: DIP shall preserve Foundation Model, AIOP, Domain Package, Workspace, and Private Strategy as distinct specialization and authority layers.

records.title: Prevent lower layers from weakening higher controls
records.normalized_publication_statement: Lower layers may specialize or strengthen authorized behavior but shall not weaken higher-order evidence, review, isolation, provenance, or human-authority controls.

records.title: Provide durable knowledge and evidence services around reasoning
records.normalized_publication_statement: DIP shall provide persistent knowledge, memory, provenance, temporal resolution, evidence management, and information isolation around model reasoning.

records.title: Keep companion intelligence governed and human-agency oriented
records.normalized_publication_statement: Companion intelligence shall improve human judgment and agency while remaining a governed participant without implicit mutation or execution authority.

records.title: Explain the operating-system framing consistently in publication
records.normalized_publication_statement: Architectural Foundations, the Blueprint, Architecture v1.0, and the Architect's Letter shall use a consistent approved explanation of DIP's category, model boundary, and durable responsibilities.

records.title: Maintain a public canonical architecture repository
records.normalized_publication_statement: The public architecture corpus shall be maintained in the authoritative dip-architecture repository after owner and revision binding.

records.title: Maintain a separately authenticated restricted evidence repository
records.normalized_publication_statement: Restricted architecture evidence shall reside in a separately authenticated location outside the public repository under explicit owner, access, and revision controls.

records.title: Prevent restricted-source loading in public builds
records.normalized_publication_statement: Public builds shall be deny-by-default and technically independent of all restricted, Private Authority, and secret-bearing locations.

records.title: Exclude actual Private Authority state from architecture repositories
records.normalized_publication_statement: Neither architecture repository shall contain or become authoritative for real instance state or active private configuration.

records.title: Prohibit secrets from both architecture repositories
records.normalized_publication_statement: All architecture and evidence repositories and their build products shall exclude secret values and shall reject evidence packages that fail secret review.

records.title: Keep complete ChatGPT exports transient
records.normalized_publication_statement: Complete account exports shall be treated as transient local intake and shall not enter either architecture repository or any publication archive.

records.title: Extract, classify, and hash only allowlisted DIP conversations
records.normalized_publication_statement: Conversation evidence intake shall be allowlisted, exact, hash-addressed, classified, and reviewed before restricted persistence or public normalization.

records.title: Bind evidence and releases to stable locators
records.normalized_publication_statement: Every release-grade normative record shall resolve through stable hash-bearing source and approval locators to immutable repository and release revisions.

records.title: Constrain public ADR projections to minimum necessary disclosure
records.normalized_publication_statement: A public ADR projection shall preserve the stable identifier, approved title, lifecycle status, abstract decision, and public-safe consequences while excluding approval quotes, restricted source identifiers, protected topology, provider and path details, private bindings, and any implication that a real protected instance exists.

records.title: Retain complete ADR bodies only within the restricted architecture boundary
records.normalized_publication_statement: Complete normalized ADR bodies, detailed topology, and restricted evidence shall reside only in the separately authenticated restricted architecture repository; that repository shall still exclude actual instance state, memberships, bindings, active private configuration, and secret values.

records.title: Validate every public structured register against a closed dedicated schema
records.normalized_publication_statement: Every public structured-register artifact shall validate against its registered Draft 2020-12 schema, reject undeclared fields, and contain only the fields explicitly approved for that projection family.

records.title: Require semantic and security review after schema validation
records.normalized_publication_statement: No structurally valid projection may be approved publicly until semantic disclosure review, prohibited-field scanning, private-instance non-disclosure review, secret scanning, and public relationship resolution all pass.

records.title: Omit failed projections without redaction fallback
records.normalized_publication_statement: When a structured register cannot produce a safe approved projection, the public build shall omit that register, retain or open a publication gap, and shall not load a complete canonical or restricted record for sanitization or redaction.
